meta.label: Board Game
meta.name: Draftosaurus
meta.short_description: A dinosaur park will always attract tourists – if you can place your species right.

boardgamehonor.name: 2019 Golden Geek Best Family Board Game Nominee
2019 Palme d'Or for Best Family Game
2020 As d'Or - Jeu de l'Année Nominee
2020 Origins Awards Best Family Game Nominee
2021 Gra Roku Children's Game of the Year Nominee
2021 Gra Roku Children's Game of the Year Winner

meta.description:
Your goal in Draftosaurus is to have the dino park most likely to attract visitors. To do so, you have to draft dino meeples and place them in pens that have some placement restrictions. Each turn, one of the players roll a die and this adds a constraint to which pens any other player can add their dinosaur.

Draftosaurus is a quick and light drafting game in which you don't have a hand of cards that you pass around (after selecting one), but a bunch of dino meeples in the palm of your hand.

COMPONENTS:
5 Zoo (player) boards (18.4x18.4cm)
60 Dinosaurs (wooden): 10x each different species red T-Rex, blue Stegosaurus, yellow Triceratops, green Parasaurolophus, orange Spinosaurus, pink Diplodocus)
1 Placement die (19mm six-sided natural wood, custom)
1 cloth draw bag
Rulebook (8 p.).
